name: Kura Oncology, Inc.
business: Kura Oncology, Inc. is a biopharmaceutical company committed to realizing the promise of precision medicines for the treatment of cancer. Its first approved product is KOMZIFTI (ziftomenib) for adults with relapsed or refractory NPM1-mutated AML. The company also has a pipeline of product candidates including darlifarnib and tipifarnib.

exposures.sentence: We are a biopharmaceutical company committed to realizing the promise of precision medicines for the treatment of cancer.

If our information technology systems, or those of third parties with whom we work, or our data are or were compromised, we could experience adverse consequences resulting from such compromise, including but not limited to regulatory investigations or actions; litigation; fines and penalties; disruptions of our business operations; reputational harm; loss of revenue or profits; and other adverse consequences.

We continue to make progress toward multiple next-generation menin inhibitor drug candidates. We have nominated our first next-generation menin inhibitor, KO-7246, which we expect to advance into IND-enabling studies in diabetes and cardiometabolic diseases in 2026 with external investment or through a collaboration.

catalysts.event: Initiated Phase 1b dose expansion cohorts of darlifarnib and cabozantinib in patients with advanced RCC
catalysts.sentence: We initiated the Phase 1b dose expansion cohorts of darlifarnib and cabozantinib in patients with advanced RCC in February 2026.

catalysts.event: Presentation of updated data for ziftomenib + 7+3 in frontline AML from KOMET-007 (first half 2026)
catalysts.sentence: We expect to present updated data evaluating the combination of ziftomenib with 7+3 in newly diagnosed NPM1-mutated or KMT2A-rearranged AML from the KOMET-007 trial in the first half of 2026.

catalysts.event: Presentation of updated data for ziftomenib + venetoclax/azacitidine in R/R NPM1-mutated AML (first half 2026)
catalysts.sentence: We expect to present updated data from this cohort in the first half of 2026.

catalysts.event: Presentation of preliminary clinical data for darlifarnib + adagrasib from FIT-001 (first half 2026)
catalysts.sentence: We anticipate the presentation of preliminary clinical data from the dose escalation portion of the FIT-001 trial evaluating the combination of darlifarnib and adagrasib in the first half of 2026.

catalysts.event: Presentation of updated Phase 1a dose-escalation data for darlifarnib + cabozantinib (second half 2026)
catalysts.sentence: We expect to present updated Phase 1a dose-escalation data from the combination in the second half of 2026.

catalysts.event: Presentation of preliminary data for ziftomenib + gilteritinib in co-mutated AML (second half 2026)
catalysts.sentence: We have completed patient enrollment in the dose expansion portion of this cohort and anticipate presenting preliminary data in the second half of 2026.

catalysts.event: Topline results from MRD-negative CR accelerated endpoint in KOMET-017-IC (intensive chemotherapy) (2028)
catalysts.sentence: We anticipate topline results from the MRD-negative CR accelerated endpoint in the intensive chemotherapy setting in 2028.

catalysts.event: Advance KO-7246 into IND-enabling studies in diabetes and cardiometabolic diseases (2026)
catalysts.sentence: We have nominated our first next-generation menin inhibitor, KO-7246, which we expect to advance into IND-enabling studies in diabetes and cardiometabolic diseases in 2026 with external investment or through a collaboration.

catalysts.event: Publication of preclinical data on menin inhibitors in diabetes (2026)
catalysts.sentence: We anticipate the publication of preclinical data on the use of menin inhibitors in diabetes in 2026.

catalysts.event: Advance preclinical development of next-generation menin inhibitor for solid tumors (2026)
catalysts.sentence: We expect to advance preclinical development of an additional next-generation menin inhibitor development candidate for use in combination therapy for solid tumors in 2026.
